root_cause: refactor left dead imports in risk module
evidence: cargo check reported unused HashSet/VecDeque and SystemTime/UNIX_EPOCH imports in crates/forex-core/src/domain/risk.rs
summary: Unused imports in forex-core risk module
recommended_fix: Remove unused imports and rerun cargo check

root_cause: portfolio code kept legacy state after refactor
evidence: cargo check reported unused DMatrix/DVector imports and dead correlation_threshold field in crates/forex-core/src/domain/portfolio.rs
summary: Unused imports and dead field in forex-core portfolio module
recommended_fix: Remove unused imports and either use or delete the dead field

root_cause: logging imports left behind after code path changes
evidence: cargo check reported unused info import in crates/forex-news/src/openai.rs and crates/forex-news/src/perplexity.rs
summary: Unused imports in forex-news providers
recommended_fix: Remove unused logging imports

root_cause: feature module still carries pre-refactor imports
evidence: cargo check reported unused Ohlcv, SymbolDataset, HashMap, and bail imports in crates/forex-data/src/core/features.rs
summary: Unused imports in forex-data feature module
recommended_fix: Prune unused imports and rerun cargo check

root_cause: validation module has stale helper imports
evidence: cargo check reported unused ArrayView1 and HashSet imports in crates/forex-search/src/validation.rs
summary: Unused imports in forex-search validation module
recommended_fix: Remove unused imports or wire them into the implementation

root_cause: model orchestration still contains migration leftovers and dormant code paths
evidence: cargo check reported 28 warnings across tree models, orchestrator, evolution, RL, streaming, and statistical modules
summary: Large cluster of unused imports, variables, and dead model fields in forex-models
recommended_fix: Remove dead imports/variables and decide whether dormant model surfaces remain supported

root_cause: bindings still use older PyO3 APIs and carry unused glue imports
evidence: cargo check reported 16 warnings across utils.rs, models.rs, data.rs, evaluation.rs, validation.rs, and executor.rs, including deprecated downcast calls
summary: Unused imports and deprecated PyO3 downcast usage in forex-bindings
recommended_fix: Remove unused imports and replace deprecated downcast calls with the supported cast API

root_cause: desktop app still has cleanup work after migration
evidence: cargo check reported unused Arc/Mutex imports and deprecated downcast usage in crates/forex-app/src/main.rs
summary: Unused imports and deprecated PyO3 downcast usage in forex-app
recommended_fix: Prune unused imports and update deprecated PyO3 calls

root_cause: distribute_gpu_assignment() uses zero-based modulo arithmetic, but the test contract is written with one-based model numbering (Model 1 -> GPU 0), so implementation and test disagree on indexing semantics
recommended_fix: Normalize model_idx before modulo or rewrite the contract and test to explicit zero-based indexing
summary: GPU distribution test fails because the implementation/test indexing contract disagrees
evidence: cargo test --workspace failed in hardware::tests::test_gpu_distribution with left=1 and right=0

summary: Multiple clippy errors in risk module
evidence: cargo clippy reported manual_range_contains, collapsible_if, too_many_arguments, manual_clamp, and derivable_impls in crates/forex-core/src/domain/risk.rs
root_cause: risk logic still contains pre-refactor complexity and style debt
recommended_fix: Split the function contracts or introduce helper structs, then replace the manual patterns with the clippy-preferred forms

summary: Needless range loop and dead portfolio state in portfolio module
evidence: cargo clippy reported dead correlation_threshold field, unused imports, and needless_range_loop in crates/forex-core/src/domain/portfolio.rs
root_cause: portfolio module retains migration-era loops and state
recommended_fix: Remove dead state and rewrite the loop with iterators/enumerate

summary: HardwareProbe lacks Default and uses needless borrows
evidence: cargo clippy reported new_without_default and needless_borrows_for_generic_args in crates/forex-core/src/system.rs
root_cause: system probe API was implemented without clippy-compatible ergonomics
recommended_fix: Add Default for HardwareProbe and pass arrays directly to args()

summary: Feature module still carries unused imports and a manual Default impl
evidence: cargo clippy reported unused imports and derivable_impls in crates/forex-data/src/core/features.rs
root_cause: feature extraction code still has refactor leftovers
recommended_fix: Drop unused imports and derive Default on FeatureProfile

summary: Needless range loop in indicator calculation
evidence: cargo clippy reported needless_range_loop in crates/forex-data/src/core/indicators.rs
root_cause: indicator computation still uses indexed loops from the Python port
recommended_fix: Rewrite the loop to use iterators or slices with enumerate

summary: Nested if can be collapsed in resample logic
evidence: cargo clippy reported collapsible_if in crates/forex-data/src/core/resample.rs
root_cause: resample path keeps layered branching from the migration
recommended_fix: Collapse the conditional and keep the logic explicit

summary: Unnecessary map_or in parquet filter
evidence: cargo clippy reported unnecessary_map_or in crates/forex-data/src/lib.rs
root_cause: file filtering still uses an older Rust pattern
recommended_fix: Replace map_or(false, ...) with is_some_and(...)

command: target/debug/forex-cli.exe discover --root data --symbol EURUSD --base M1 --higher M5,M15,H1 --population 10 --generations 1 --candidates 20 --portfolio-size 10
summary: Discovery CLI reports success even when it produces an empty portfolio
evidence: runtime probe exited 0 with output `Discovery EURUSD portfolio=0 candidates=3 out=cache/talib_knowledge.json`
root_cause: the CLI always saves and prints the discovery result without elevating an empty portfolio to a degraded or failed outcome
recommended_fix: Surface an explicit degraded-state warning or non-zero exit when discovery yields no portfolio so operators can distinguish success from unusable output

summary: UI discovery tab is not connected to the real backend
evidence: crates/forex-app/src/main.rs spawns a mock progress loop with tokio::sleep and never calls forex-search or any discovery service
root_cause: the desktop UI still contains placeholder discovery behavior from the migration phase
recommended_fix: Replace the mock loop with a real discovery task contract and explicit progress/result propagation from the Rust backend

summary: UI training tab does not invoke any training backend
evidence: crates/forex-app/src/main.rs only logs `Training logic triggered.` when the training button is clicked
root_cause: the training UI surface was scaffolded without a bound engine/orchestrator action
recommended_fix: Wire the button to a concrete training command path and propagate success/failure/progress back into UI state

summary: Local app mode ignores configured data roots and hardcodes `data`
evidence: crates/forex-app/src/main.rs uses forex_data::discover_symbols("data") in headless mode, constructor initialization, and refresh logic
root_cause: UI/local-mode data access is bypassing runtime configuration and assumes a single repo-local data directory
recommended_fix: Resolve the data root from config/runtime state once and pass it through the UI/app lifecycle instead of hardcoding `data`

summary: MT5 bridge mis-types `last_error()` and can mask the real failure cause
evidence: crates/mt5-bridge/src/lib.rs extracts `last_error()` as String, while the official MQL5 Python docs say `last_error()` returns the error code and description as a tuple: https://www.mql5.com/en/docs/python_metatrader5/mt5lasterror_py
root_cause: the bridge assumes a scalar string error contract instead of the tuple contract exposed by the MetaTrader5 Python API
recommended_fix: Extract the return value as a tuple or structured object and surface both code and description without triggering a PyO3 type-conversion error

summary: Dataset loader silently drops timeframes that fail to load
evidence: crates/forex-data/src/lib.rs wraps load_symbol_timeframe() in `if let Ok(...)` inside load_symbol_dataset() and returns a partial dataset without surfacing the failed timeframe
root_cause: the data-loading contract treats failed timeframe loads as optional instead of explicit errors or degraded-state events
recommended_fix: Track and report failed timeframe loads explicitly, or fail the dataset build when a required timeframe cannot be loaded

summary: Targeted dataset loader repeats the same silent-drop behavior for requested timeframes
evidence: crates/forex-data/src/lib.rs uses the same `if let Ok(...)` pattern in load_symbol_dataset_with_timeframes() and silently omits requested frames on failure
root_cause: requested timeframe loads are treated as best-effort even when the caller explicitly asked for them
recommended_fix: Return structured missing-frame errors for requested timeframes instead of silently omitting them

summary: Parquet timestamp casting can silently fabricate epoch-zero timestamps
evidence: crates/forex-data/src/lib.rs falls back to `Series::new(..., vec![0i64])` when timestamp casting fails, then iterates those zeros into the returned timestamp vector
root_cause: timestamp parse/cast failures are converted into synthetic default data instead of load errors
recommended_fix: Fail the parquet load on timestamp cast errors or propagate a structured missing-timestamp state instead of fabricating zero timestamps

summary: Public MLP binding is a placeholder rather than a usable model API
evidence: crates/forex-bindings/src/models.rs declares `pub struct MLPModel { pub inner: Mutex<Gene> } // Placeholder for MLP using Gene` and only exposes a constructor in this module
root_cause: migration scaffolding exported a placeholder model class into the public bindings surface before a real implementation contract existed
recommended_fix: Either remove the public placeholder from the bindings surface or replace it with a real model wrapper exposing fit/predict/save-load methods

summary: Public GeneticModel binding is exported with constructor-only behavior
evidence: crates/forex-bindings/src/models.rs registers `GeneticModel` publicly, but this module only exposes `new()` and no operational methods
root_cause: the public bindings surface exposes a skeletal class without a complete runtime contract
recommended_fix: Expose the full operational API for GeneticModel or stop exporting it until the binding is complete

summary: Training runtime path can report success without training any model
evidence: crates/forex-models/src/training_orchestrator.rs passes a closure to train_models_parallel() that only logs and returns `Ok(())`, while the CLI runtime path still prints `Pure Rust training complete for {symbol}`
root_cause: the Rust training orchestrator still contains placeholder implementation code in the actual runtime path
recommended_fix: Replace the placeholder closure with real model dispatch, fit, and persistence logic before treating the Rust training path as production-ready

summary: Training orchestrator ignores configuration-driven model selection
evidence: crates/forex-models/src/training_orchestrator.rs hardcodes `vec!["xgboost", "genetic"]` in get_enabled_models() instead of deriving the enabled model set from settings
root_cause: model orchestration was scaffolded with a fixed list rather than being wired to the loaded config contract
recommended_fix: Drive enabled-model selection from the parsed settings surface and validate unknown/unsupported model names explicitly

summary: Walk-forward backtest can panic on zero splits
evidence: crates/forex-search/src/validation.rs computes `let window = (n / n_splits).max(1);` without validating that `n_splits > 0`
root_cause: input validation is missing on a public backtest parameter before integer division
recommended_fix: Reject zero splits explicitly with a Result error before any division or loop setup

summary: Walk-forward compliance flag ignores the caller-provided loss threshold
evidence: crates/forex-search/src/validation.rs sets `prop_compliant: max_daily_dd < 0.05` while also accepting `max_daily_loss_pct` as a parameter
root_cause: the function mixes a hardcoded compliance rule with a configurable risk-threshold contract
recommended_fix: Compute `prop_compliant` from the supplied threshold (or a clearly named policy object) so the output matches the input contract

summary: Walk-forward summary returns placeholder risk/compliance fields
evidence: crates/forex-search/src/validation.rs sets `max_consec_losses: 0`, `consistency_violation: false`, `trade_limit_violation: false`, and `min_trading_days_ok: true` with `Simplified for now` comments
root_cause: the public summary type exposes richer semantics than the current implementation computes
recommended_fix: Either compute the real values or remove/mark these fields as unavailable so downstream consumers do not trust fabricated defaults

summary: Portfolio optimizer ignores its public lookback_days parameter
evidence: crates/forex-search/src/portfolio.rs stores `lookback_days` on the optimizer but never uses it when slicing returns before allocation
root_cause: the optimizer API kept the historical lookback parameter while the implementation moved to full-series calculations
recommended_fix: Apply the lookback window consistently to the return series or remove the parameter from the public contract

summary: Discovery ignores caller requests for candidate counts below 100
evidence: crates/forex-search/src/discovery.rs forces `let max_candidates = config.candidate_count.max(100).min(candidates.len())`, so any configured count below 100 is silently raised
root_cause: the discovery implementation enforces a hidden lower bound instead of honoring or validating the public candidate_count contract
recommended_fix: Either respect the supplied candidate_count exactly or reject invalid low values explicitly instead of silently overriding them

summary: Batch discovery can return success after skipping all failing symbols and timeframes
evidence: crates/forex-search/src/orchestration.rs converts dataset-load, resample, and feature-prep errors into `info!` logs plus `continue`, then ends with unconditional `Ok(())`
root_cause: batch orchestration treats per-symbol and per-timeframe failures as informational events without aggregating degraded-state outcomes for the caller
recommended_fix: Track skipped/failing work units and return an explicit degraded or failed result when nothing usable was processed

summary: Feature-loading binding ignores advertised feature-budget and HTF-profile controls
evidence: crates/forex-bindings/src/data.rs discards `max_features`, `max_htf_features`, and the parsed `htf_feature_profile` via local `_` bindings before building features
root_cause: the binding signature was expanded ahead of the actual feature-building implementation
recommended_fix: Wire the public parameters into FeatureBuildOptions or remove them until the binding can honor the contract

summary: Public strategy-signal loader is an empty placeholder
evidence: crates/forex-bindings/src/data.rs exports `load_strategy_signals()` and returns `PyDict::new(py)` without loading anything
root_cause: migration scaffolding left a public binding entrypoint in place before the backing implementation existed
recommended_fix: Implement the real signal-loading bridge or remove the public function from the bindings surface until it is ready

summary: Multiple exported data-alignment and news helpers are still hardcoded stubs
evidence: crates/forex-bindings/src/data.rs registers `count_weekday_trading_days`, `align_ffill_values_by_ns`, `align_exact_values_by_ns`, `align_feature_matrix`, `sorted_index_order`, `aggregate_news_features`, and `aggregate_news_activation`; the first always returns `0` and the rest return `None`
root_cause: the public PyO3 module exports placeholder helper functions instead of implemented bridge logic
recommended_fix: Remove the stubbed functions from the public bindings module or replace them with real implementations before external callers depend on them

summary: Search bindings silently replace serialization failures with None results
evidence: crates/forex-bindings/src/search.rs uses `pythonize(py, g).map(|obj| obj.into()).unwrap_or_else(|_| py.None())` in both `search_evolve_ohlcv()` and `search_discovery_ohlcv()`
root_cause: result serialization failures are treated as best-effort payload shaping instead of binding errors
recommended_fix: Propagate Pythonization failures explicitly so callers cannot mistake partially-null result payloads for successful search output

summary: GPU discovery binding always reports GPU execution even on non-GPU builds
evidence: crates/forex-bindings/src/search.rs sets `dict.set_item("gpu", true)` unconditionally while also compiling a `#[cfg(not(feature = "gpu"))]` execution path
root_cause: the result payload hardcodes a capability flag instead of reporting the actual build/runtime execution mode
recommended_fix: Set the returned GPU flag from the active runtime path or remove it until the binding can report execution mode accurately

summary: Python walk-forward binding ignores caller-supplied backtest settings
evidence: crates/forex-bindings/src/validation.rs discards the `settings` argument with `let _ = settings` and always uses `BacktestSettings::default()`
root_cause: the binding signature exposes a configurable settings object that was never wired into the Rust validator
recommended_fix: Parse and forward the supplied settings object, or remove the parameter until the binding can honor it

summary: Trade journal metrics binding is a hardcoded placeholder
evidence: crates/forex-bindings/src/evaluation.rs returns a dict with `sharpe=0.0` and `win_rate=0.0` regardless of the supplied journal data
root_cause: placeholder analytics code remained exported as a production metric endpoint
recommended_fix: Implement the real journal-metrics calculation or remove the public binding until it is available

summary: Parallel trainer suppresses model failures behind a successful return value
evidence: crates/forex-models/src/parallel_trainer.rs collects failures in a local vector, logs them, and still returns `Ok(successes)`
root_cause: the parallel training helper models partial failure as logging-only side effects rather than an explicit result contract
recommended_fix: Return structured success and failure sets or fail the overall call when any required model training fails

summary: ONNX model loader reports success when no model directories exist
evidence: crates/forex-models/src/lib.rs logs warnings for missing models or missing `onnx/` directory and then returns `Ok(())`
root_cause: the inference loader treats missing assets as non-fatal without exposing that degraded state to its caller
recommended_fix: Return an explicit degraded/empty-load result or an error when the requested ONNX model directory is absent

summary: ONNX inference hardcodes a fixed four-thread session budget
evidence: crates/forex-models/src/lib.rs builds sessions with `.with_intra_threads(4)` and `.with_inter_threads(4)` regardless of detected hardware or runtime thread budget
root_cause: the ONNX runtime integration was configured with fixed thread counts instead of the repo-wide adaptive CPU budget
recommended_fix: Derive ONNX thread counts from the shared hardware/runtime budget or make them explicit configuration inputs

summary: Training CLI advertises a root flag that it never uses
evidence: crates/forex-cli/src/main.rs parses `--root` into `_root` inside cmd_train() and never forwards it to the training orchestrator
root_cause: the CLI surface kept the data-root option while the Rust training orchestrator still resolves the root independently
recommended_fix: Pass the CLI root into the training path explicitly or remove the flag from the command contract

summary: CLI help documents unsupported train flags
evidence: crates/forex-cli/src/main.rs prints `train --symbol EURUSD --base M1 --higher H1,H4 --horizon 1 --root data`, but cmd_train() only parses `--symbol`, `--base`, `--models-dir`, and `--config`
root_cause: the human-facing command help drifted after the training command surface changed
recommended_fix: Regenerate or hand-update the CLI help text so it matches the actually parsed flags

summary: OpenAI news scorer turns missing credentials into a neutral sentiment result
evidence: crates/forex-news/src/openai.rs logs `OPENAI_API_KEY not set` and then returns `Ok(0.0)`
root_cause: provider-unavailable state is collapsed into a plausible neutral score instead of a structured unavailable/degraded response
recommended_fix: Return an explicit unavailable/degraded result when provider credentials are missing so callers can distinguish configuration issues from real neutral sentiment

summary: Perplexity news search collapses missing credentials into an empty-news result
evidence: crates/forex-news/src/perplexity.rs logs `PERPLEXITY_API_KEY not set` and then returns `Ok(String::new())`
root_cause: provider-unavailable state is represented as an empty successful payload rather than a structured degraded result
recommended_fix: Return an explicit unavailable/degraded result when provider credentials are missing so callers do not confuse missing configuration with no-news conditions

summary: Logging flush guard is dropped immediately after setup
evidence: crates/forex-core/src/logging.rs stores the `WorkerGuard` in a local `_guard`; the official tracing-appender docs state that `WorkerGuard` must be assigned to a binding that is not `_` or the flush guarantee is lost: https://docs.rs/tracing-appender/latest/tracing_appender/non_blocking/
root_cause: logging setup returns after creating the non-blocking writer without keeping the guard alive at the application entrypoint
recommended_fix: Return the WorkerGuard to the caller or store it in application state so buffered logs are flushed reliably on shutdown or panic

summary: Active app entrypoint bypasses the structured logging module
evidence: repo search shows crates/forex-app/src/main.rs calls `tracing_subscriber::fmt::init()` directly, while `forex-core/src/logging.rs` is not used by the active app path
root_cause: the desktop app boot path was wired independently from the shared logging contract
recommended_fix: Route app startup through the shared logging setup contract or delete the unused logging layer and define one authoritative logging initialization path

summary: Logging comment claims rotation that the implementation does not provide
evidence: crates/forex-core/src/logging.rs documents `50MB max, 3 backups`, but the code uses tracing_appender::rolling::never(...)
root_cause: documentation was carried forward from an earlier logging design while the implementation was simplified
recommended_fix: Either implement the advertised rotation policy or update the comment and operator docs to the actual retention behavior
